Based on my visual inspection of the molecule, I can now identify the neighbour atoms of atom 14 (N):

## Results:

**Atom 14:** N (Nitrogen)

**Neighbour atoms of Atom 14 (covalently connected):**

| Atomic Index | Element |
|--------------|---------|
| 13 | C (Carbon) |
| 15 | C (Carbon) |
| 55 | H (Hydrogen) |

**geomconf_index:** 4409

Atom 14 is a nitrogen atom that forms three covalent bonds - two with carbon atoms (indices 13 and 15) and one with a hydrogen atom (index 55). This is consistent with typical nitrogen bonding, where nitrogen forms three single bonds.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 14, whose element should be N. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
